replies.msg:
If you have an EU passport (or even an ID card issued by an EU country), you can enter the UK to exercise freedom of movement until Brexit in March 2019.

It would be best if you open an UK bank account in Hong Kong before moving over to the UK (我們會安排專人了解您的個人需要並協助您預先開立海外戶口及跟進整個申請程序). HSBC offers this kind of service -

https://www.hsbc.com.hk/zh-hk/personal/hsbc-premier/features/premier-overseas-account.html

After arriving in the UK, you will need to get a National Insurance number. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

Once you are a Qualify Person (an EU national exercising EEA treaty rights like working for example), go and apply for a registration certificate -

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/apply-for-a-registration-certificate-as-a-qualified-person-form-eea-qp

Before you apply for British Citizenship, you need to get permanent residence first. You can get permanent residence after 5 years as a Qualify Person. After having permanent residence for a year, you can apply for British Citizenship.

https://www.gov.uk/eea-registration-certificate/permanent-residence

感謝巴打 好詳細
但我睇certificate 果道話要有uk外既保險
咁我想問下係咪一定要cover好哂我黎緊個5年？
同埋就算讀master都係得一年 學校會唔會寫信比我去apply 同 果邊會唔會因為你得一年讀書既proof而唔批 ？

replies.msg:
但我睇certificate 果道話要有uk外既保險 - If you are an EU citizen exercising your EEA treaty rights in the UK as a student or self sufficient person, you will need to get a comprehensive sickness insurance (CSI) in order to get permanent residence in the UK.

However, if you are working, you will be paying UK's National Insurance (NI), so you do not need to get CSI.

咁我想問下係咪一定要cover好哂我黎緊個5年？ - you need to have CSI as long as you are not working and paying NI contributions in the UK.

同埋就算讀master都係得一年 - If you are going to do a master degree in the UK, you can still be charge 'overseas' fees, rather than 'Home / EEA' fees as you never lived in the EU for at lease three years before your UK master degree course starts in September 2018.

果邊會唔會因為你得一年讀書既proof而唔批 - you will get a registration certificate for 5 years. However, if you are not exercising EEA treaty rights (for example working or studying) after your master degree, you will not get permanent residence after 5 years.

replies.msg:
The current minimum wage for 21 to 24 year old people in the UK is £7.05 per hour.

https://www.gov.uk/national-minimum-wage-rates

Higher taxes in the UK -

Income tax at lease 20%
National Insurance (NI) contribution 12%

Your taxes and NI contribution will be taken 'at source' by the Pay As You Earn system. This means that the salary you received in your bank account is your net income (no need to save up and pay income tax like Hong Kong) and you can spend the last penny if you want.

quote.msg:
其實如果你有專業技能

去英國做野拿居留易好多

replies.msg:
The OP holds an EU passport (Portuguese?) and this is the easiest way to move to the UK.

其實如果你有專業技能去英國做野拿居留易好多 - a Tier 2 (General) work visa holder will still need to live in the UK for 5 years before getting permanent residence and wait a year after that before getting British Citizenship. Getting a Tier 2 (General) visa is not that easy. You will need to find an employer to sponsor you and pay a lot of money to get your Tier 2 visa.

Tier 2 (General) visa cost £892 for jobs in the shortage occupation list and £1174 for those jobs not in the shortage occupation list. The OP can get an EEA (QP) registration certificate by paying £65.

There is also a quota system for sponsorship certificates for Tier 2 visas, for example, the quota for January 2018 will be 1409, while there is no quota system in place just now for EEA citizens coming to live in the UK.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/employer-sponsorship-restricted-certificate-allocations/allocations-of-restricted-certificates-of-sponsorship

You need to remember it can take a long time to get your Tier 2 visa while the OP can just buy an air ticket and move to the UK whenever he wants.

quote.msg: BNO留名

replies.msg:
An easier way to get British Citizenship for you - join the British Armed Forces and serve for at lease 5 years.

東網透視：憑一支單簧管　香港仔入選御林軍

以香港第一人身份成爲英國御林軍樂手，David憶述，源於幾年前一次聚餐時偶遇英國陸軍音樂總監，對方提起英國御林軍樂隊招募新隊員，他持有英國國民(海外)護照(BNO)身份也可參與招聘考試，故決定一試。軍樂隊考試分兩部分，體能為先，音樂第二。對於當年33歲的他來說，體能測試是極大的挑戰，要10分半鐘內跑完2.4公里，亦要做掌上壓及仰臥起坐，首輪面試因跑步不達標而失敗。但他並沒有放棄，返港苦操3、4個月後再接受挑戰，2013年過關斬將通過所有測試，成功取錄。

身為英皇室的御林軍，薪酬福利令人關注，David則不予透露，僅指薪金水平較香港警察低一些，除普通的醫療和交通福利外，最優厚是住房福利，他與同事在倫敦市中心合住一間800至900平方呎單位，月租只需30英鎊，僅相當於300港元，相信令不少香港青年人羨慕。他現長居英國，還有一年便可入籍，目標是繼續在軍隊攀升，成爲終身職業。

http://202.125.90.17/hk/bkn/cnt/news/20170812/bkn-20170812093047180-0812_00822_001.html

Also have a look at the thread below -

BNO與其去做慈母　不如加入英軍

https://lihkg.com/thread/422251/page/1

One more thing I forgot to say - go and learn how to drive and pass your driving test in Hong Kong. You can exchange your Hong Kong driving licence to an UK one without test as long as you pass your driving test in Hong Kong.

https://www.facebook.com/HongkongerinUK/posts/861886470578180:0
入籍盧森堡之後過去會容易d

The current minimum wage for 21 to 24 year old people in the UK is £7.05 per hour.

https://www.gov.uk/national-minimum-wage-rates

Higher taxes in the UK -

Income tax at lease 20%
National Insurance (NI) contribution 12%

Your taxes and NI contribution will be taken 'at source' by the Pay As You Earn system. This means that the salary you received in your bank account is your net income (no need to save up and pay income tax like Hong Kong) and you can spend the last penny if you want.
我知起碼20%但唔知原來係直扣
你拿最低工資係好難申請居留

replies.msg:
你拿最低工資係好難申請居留 - EU citizens exercising EEA treaty rights in the UK are govern by EU law. Have a look at the link below -

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/653729/eea-free-movement-rights-guidance-v17_0ext.pdf

See page 19 on EEA permanent residence -

An EEA national is automatically entitled to live permanently in the UK once they
have lived here continuously for a period of 5 years

As long as you are exercising your EEA treaty rights, you are entitle to get UK permanent residence after 5 years.

Are you studying in the UK?

In order to apply for settle / permanent residence under EU law, you, the non British Citizen EEA national, need to exercise EEA treaty rights in the UK. If you are a student or self sufficient EEA national in the UK, you will only be seen as exercising EEA treaty rights if you have a comprehensive sickness insurance covering your time in the UK as a student or self sufficient person.

但我都唔太清楚搵工係點攞住settled status留到英,係咪攞到status之後再留幾年就咩都搞掂晒 - You can apply for British Citizenship once you have permanent residence status for at lease a year.

To apply for citizenship by naturalisation based on residence, you must prove that you have been resident in the State for at least 5 years (1825 or 1826 days) out of the last 9 years

This includes 1 year (365 or 366 days) of continuous residence immediately before the date you apply.

In a way, it is a bit easier to either register (for BN(O) passport holders only) or naturalise to become a British Citizen. You can be away from the UK for up to 90 days in the last year - unlike Ireland where you need to stay in Ireland for the full year before you apply.

replies.msg:
I do not know the way for bno passport holder to become a bc that you mentioned - you can get British Citizen as long as you have Indefinite Leave to Remain (non EEA nationals) or permanent residence (EEA nationals) in the UK.

For BN(O) holders, the most difficult thing is to get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR). You can get ILR using the following ways -

1) Service in the British Armed Forces (4 years)
2) Spouse visa (5 years)
3) Tier 2 (General) (5 years)
4) Long residence (10 years of legal residence in the UK - including student visa)

Once a BN(O) passport holder has ILR, he can apply for British Citizenship using form B(OTA), which is a cheaper way than naturalisation.

quote.msg: In a way, it is a bit easier to either register (for BN(O) passport holders only) or naturalise to become a British Citizen. You can be away from the UK for up to 90 days in the last year - unlike Ireland where you need to stay in Ireland for the full year before you apply.

I do not know the way for bno passport holder to become a bc that you mentioned, but i am going to use a EU passport to stay in the uk. The ireland way is just a suggestion for those who may not be able to enter and stay in the uk as a qualified person before the brexit, so that they may be able to be an ireland resident and stay in the uk as well.

I do not know the way for bno passport holder to become a bc that you mentioned - you can get British Citizen as long as you have Indefinite Leave to Remain (non EEA nationals) or permanent residence (EEA nationals) in the UK.

For BN(O) holders, the most difficult thing is to get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR). You can get ILR using the following ways -

1) Service in the British Armed Forces (4 years)
2) Spouse visa (5 years)
3) Tier 2 (General) (5 years)
4) Long residence (10 years of legal residence in the UK - including student visa)

Once a BN(O) passport holder has ILR, he can apply for British Citizenship using form B(OTA), which is a cheaper way than naturalisation.

replies.msg: the ILR you mentioned, which is not my plan, especially tier 2 or long residence, is it very hard for usual people? and how long people are allowed to leave the UK within this duration?

EEA citizens wishing to remain in the UK should do it before Brexit in 2019 and apply for a registration certificate from the Home Office.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/apply-for-a-registration-certificate-as-an-eea-qualified-person-form-eea-qp

You can apply for an EEA registration certificate using your national ID card (if you have one)!

replies.msg:
Identity cards
Identity cards were scrapped in 2011 - they’re no longer valid and you can’t use them as proof of identify.

You don’t have to return your identity card. You should destroy it or keep it in a safe place.

The fee you paid won’t be refunded.

The government securely destroyed the personal details of everyone who had an identity card.

You can’t use your identity card for travel. Use your passport instead. Contact the British embassy if you’re abroad without a passport.
